title: DCAT / schema.org Dataset export of the catalog
context: To make the catalog discoverable by dataset-discovery ecosystems (Google Dataset Search, OpenAIRE, etc.), each catalog record should emit a schema.org/Dataset + DCAT JSON-LD representation. This turns the catalog's machine-readable JSON records into interoperable artifacts that can be indexed by existing discovery infrastructure. The DCAT/schema.org export must map the catalog's access-tier taxonomy and license-rights breakdown into the equivalent DCAT/DCTERMS fields, preserving the access-tier signals rather than losing them in a lowest-common-denominator serialization. The export must contain only cohort-level metadata (no patient data). MIT-licensed code.
objective: Build a build-time utility that generates schema.org/Dataset + DCAT JSON-LD representations of each catalog record — mapping access tier, license rights, and provenance to standard DCAT/DCTERMS fields — so the catalog is discoverable by Google Dataset Search and similar dataset-discovery ecosystems without exposing patient data.
acceptanceCriteria: A utility script generates schema.org/Dataset + DCAT JSON-LD for each catalog record, mapping: id, name, custodian, hostingLocations, cancerTypes, accessTier (to DCAT accrualPolicy or dcterms:accessRights), license (to schema:license and dcterms:rights), provenance (to dcterms:provenance), and the catalog's custom fields as extension properties.
The DCAT/JSON-LD output preserves the access-tier signal (OPEN, OPEN-AGGREGATE, REGISTERED-OPEN) in a standard field that Google Dataset Search and similar tools can consume.
The export is generated at build time and committed alongside the catalog records; it contains only cohort-level metadata (no patient data, no per-patient rows).
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; MIT-licensed; DCO signed-off.
output: A committed utility (MIT) that generates schema.org/Dataset + DCAT JSON-LD for each catalog record, built into CI so interop representations are always current.
